prompt:
You are a B2B prospecting analyst for a web agency. Analyze this locksmith's trust signals.

Locksmiths have a massive trust problem — the industry is plagued by scam operators. Trust signals are critical for conversion.

HOT LEAD signals:
- No reviews or testimonials on site
- No mention of license or insurance
- No trade body membership (MLA, ALOA)
- No guarantee mentioned
- Stock photos of locks instead of real team
- No Google Reviews integration

COLD LEAD signals:
- Embedded Google/Trustpilot reviews
- Trade body logos (Master Locksmiths Association)
- License and insurance numbers
- Named team members with photos
- Guarantee/warranty stated
- Case studies or before/after photos
- Years in business prominently displayed

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of trust signals>",
  "opportunityScore": "<1-10, higher = fewer trust signals = better lead>",
  "hasReviews": true/false,
  "hasTradeBody": true/false,
  "hasInsurance": true/false,
  "hasNamedTeam": true/false,
  "hasGuarantee": true/false,
  "topOpportunity": "<what a web agency could sell here>",
  "notes": "<one sentence prospecting assessment>"
}

---
You are analyzing web pages for the category described above.
For each page provided below, extract one record.
Follow the JSON schema from the category description exactly.

## Required fields (always include)
- "url": the page URL
- "pageName": short human-readable page name
- "summary": 1-2 sentence plain-language overview

## Field naming conventions (follow strictly)
- Scores: name ends with "Score", value is a number 1-10 (e.g. "conversionScore": 7)
- Booleans: name starts with "has"/"is" or ends with "Visible" (e.g. "pricingVisible": true)
- Short text: "notes", "description" — 1-2 sentences max
- Tags/lists: arrays of short strings (e.g. "classStyles": ["Hatha", "Vinyasa"])
- Plain strings: everything else (e.g. "price": "€15 drop-in")

Return a JSON object: { "records": [<one record per page>] }.
No markdown, no code fences.
response:
{
  "records": [
    {
      "url": "https://wdlocksmiths.co.uk/testimonials/",
      "pageName": "Watchdog Locksmiths – Testimonials",
      "summary": "The page features named written testimonials with first/last names and a video case study from a named customer, showing genuine social proof. However, there is no embedded Google or Trustpilot widget, no trade body logos, no insurance/licence numbers, and no guarantee mentioned.",
      "opportunityScore": 5,
      "hasReviews": true,
      "hasTradeBody": false,
      "hasInsurance": false,
      "hasNamedTeam": true,
      "hasGuarantee": false,
      "topOpportunity": "Integrate an embedded Google Reviews or Trustpilot widget to replace manually curated testimonials with independently verified social proof, significantly boosting credibility.",
      "notes": "A moderately trusted site with genuine-feeling testimonials and named technicians, but the absence of third-party verified reviews, trade body membership, and licence/insurance details leaves meaningful trust gaps a web agency could address."
    },
    {
      "url": "https://wdlocksmiths.co.uk/our-partners/",
      "pageName": "Watchdog Locksmiths – Our Partners",
      "summary": "The page showcases a strong roster of institutional partners including the NHS, Metropolitan Police, Ealing Council, and Hammersmith & Fulham, which are powerful credibility signals. Despite this, no MLA or ALOA trade body accreditation logos are visible, and no insurance or licence numbers are displayed.",
      "opportunityScore": 4,
      "hasReviews": false,
      "hasTradeBody": false,
      "hasInsurance": false,
      "hasNamedTeam": false,
      "hasGuarantee": false,
      "topOpportunity": "Add a dedicated accreditations section featuring MLA membership, insurance certificate details, and licence numbers to complement the impressive partner logos already present.",
      "notes": "High-calibre institutional partnerships give this page genuine authority, but the lack of recognised locksmith trade body badges and verifiable credentials means the trust story is incomplete — a targeted accreditation and compliance module would round it out."
    }
  ]
}
